question: Will CPI rise more than 0.2% in May 2026?
reasoning: Recent CPI data shows monthly increases of 0.2% (January) and 0.3% (February 2026), indicating moderate inflation momentum. However, LongForecast predicts May 2026 CPI to decline by -0.074 points to 2.26% annually, suggesting deflationary pressures. RBC Economics expects core CPI to peak in Q2 2026 at 3% due to tariff effects, but shelter inflation is expected to slow significantly as housing market weakness feeds through with a ~2-year lag. The combination of seasonal factors, moderating shelter costs, and base effects from prior year comparisons suggests roughly even odds of exceeding 0.2% monthly growth in May 2026.
key_factors: Recent monthly CPI increases of 0.2% (Jan) and 0.3% (Feb) showing current momentum
LongForecast predicting May 2026 CPI decline of -0.074 points
RBC expects core CPI peak in Q2 2026 at 3% from tariff passthrough
Shelter inflation expected to moderate as housing market weakness feeds through
Seasonal adjustment factors for May could impact monthly calculation
risks: Tariff effects could create upward price pressure in Q2 2026
Energy price volatility from geopolitical factors
Missing October 2025 data could cause measurement distortions in spring 2026
Food price increases from cattle supply constraints
Potential fiscal stimulus could boost demand and prices
